plan.topic: Restructure codex-nexus repo to stop tracking project-scope install artifacts in .codex/.agents

issues.title: Separate publishable plugin sources from project-scope install artifacts so repo-root .codex/.agents no longer need to be tracked while sync, validate, and install guarantees remain intact.
issues.decision: Selected approach: treat plugins/codex-nexus/** as the only tracked publishable plugin source, stop using repo-root .codex/.agents as tracked validation fixtures, narrow sync-from-nexus-core.mjs to sync publishable plugin assets only, and move install-behavior verification to tests that create temporary installs. Update validation and layout tests so they verify publishable assets plus installer behavior instead of requiring tracked repo-root .codex/.agents. Rejected alternative A: keep the current mixed model and only untrack .codex/config.toml and .codex/hooks.json; dismissed because .agents/.codex would remain semantically ambiguous and repo self-install artifacts would still leak into source control. Rejected alternative B: keep tracked fixtures but move them to test/fixtures/project-install; dismissed because stable fixture snapshots would still duplicate installer behavior already covered by installer tests and would add more sync surface to maintain.

plan.research_summary: Inspected scripts/sync-from-nexus-core.mjs, scripts/validate-plugin.mjs, test/plugin-layout.test.js, test/installer.test.js, README, package manifest, and current git state. Findings: (1) plugins/codex-nexus/** is the publishable source of truth; package.files excludes .codex and .agents. (2) Repo-root .codex/.agents are currently treated as tracked generated fixtures: sync-from-nexus-core.mjs writes .codex/agents, .codex/lead.instructions.md, .codex/config.toml, and .agents/skills into the repo root. (4) .codex/config.toml, .codex/hooks.json, and .codex/packages are local install outputs and create git noise; current branch already has local modifications there. (5) No prior Nexus history exists for this topic.

plan.topic: Fix recurring subagent nx MCP startup failure in codex-nexus installs

issues.title: Identify the precise root cause and authoritative fix shape for subagent nx MCP configuration

analysis.summary: Root cause is a configuration mismatch between the main Codex session and spawned subagents. The installer writes `.codex/config.toml` so the main session launches the `nx` MCP server through the current runtime plus an absolute `@moreih29/nexus-core/dist/mcp/server.js` path, explicitly avoiding PATH-dependent commands. But every generated non-lead subagent TOML overrides `[mcp_servers.nx]` with `command = "nexus-mcp"`, which requires a bare executable on PATH. In this repo, `command -v nexus-mcp` is empty, matching the observed `MCP startup failed: No such file or directory (os error 2)`. Official Codex docs say MCP servers are configured under `config.toml`, and custom agent files are configuration layers; optional fields like `mcp_servers` inherit from the parent when omitted. Public Codex issue reports (#2945, #4754) show the same class of failure when the configured MCP command cannot be launched.

issues.decision: Selected approach: treat the failure as a bad subagent MCP launcher configuration, not as an nx MCP server defect. The authoritative fix should make subagent agent files launch `nx` with a resolvable command instead of bare `nexus-mcp`, while preserving the existing `disabled_tools` restrictions for each role. Rationale: official Codex docs define MCP launch through `mcp_servers.<id>.command/args` in config, and the current installer already uses a concrete runtime + absolute server path for the main session; the bug appears only because spawned subagents override that with an unresolved command. Rejected alternatives: (1) ask users to install `nexus-mcp` globally or patch PATH — rejected because it contradicts the package’s documented promise to avoid PATH dependence and would remain fragile across environments; (2) leave source files unchanged and only document the limitation — rejected because the failure is deterministic and breaks a core workflow; (3) remove subagent `mcp_servers.nx` sections entirely and rely on inheritance — rejected because it would also remove current per-role `disabled_tools` restrictions unless we rely on subtler merge behavior than needed.

issues.title: Implement the code change so installed subagents no longer depend on a missing bare `nexus-mcp` executable

analysis.summary: The safest repair is two-stage normalization. First, normalize tracked plugin agent specs after `sync:core` so they no longer ship a bare `nexus-mcp` command; instead they should use the same pinned package-based launcher shape as the plugin `.mcp.json` (`command = "npx"` plus `-y -p @moreih29/nexus-core@<version> nexus-mcp`). Second, during `codex-nexus install`, rewrite the copied agent TOMLs in both the installed plugin copy and `.codex/agents` to the already-proven local-runtime shape (`command = <process.execPath>`, `args = [<absolute dist/mcp/server.js>]`). This preserves disabled tool lists, keeps publishable source self-consistent, and preserves the installer’s stronger PATH-independent guarantee for real user installs.

issues.decision: Selected implementation: introduce shared helper logic that rewrites the `[mcp_servers.nx]` block in generated agent TOMLs while preserving each file’s `disabled_tools`. Use that helper in two places: (a) `sync-from-nexus-core.mjs` to rewrite publishable plugin agent specs to a pinned `npx -y -p @moreih29/nexus-core@<version> nexus-mcp` launcher, and (b) `codex-nexus.mjs` install flow to rewrite copied agent specs to the stronger runtime-specific launcher (`process.execPath` + absolute installed `dist/mcp/server.js` path). Also teach install health checks to catch regressions. Rationale: this matches official Codex MCP configuration semantics, aligns source/plugin/install layers, and avoids reliance on a globally installed executable. Rejected alternatives: (1) patch only `.codex/config.toml` — rejected because subagent agent files currently override the server and would still win; (2) patch only source plugin files to `npx` and stop there — rejected because it still leaves installed subagents PATH-dependent when the installer already promises a stronger invariant; (3) patch only installed `.codex/agents` and ignore plugin copies/source — rejected because it leaves the package’s publishable artifacts internally inconsistent and easier to regress on the next sync.

issues.title: Add regression coverage so future syncs or releases do not reintroduce PATH-dependent subagent MCP launch behavior

analysis.summary: Current tests verify the main `.codex/config.toml` uses a resolved server path, but they never assert anything about installed subagent TOMLs. That gap allowed the regression. Coverage should assert both layers: source plugin agent specs should not contain `command = "nexus-mcp"`, and installed `.codex/agents/*.toml` should inherit the installer’s runtime-specific command plus absolute server path. A lightweight doctor/validation check can fail fast on broken installs before the user reaches a subagent flow.

issues.decision: Selected regression strategy: expand automated tests and lightweight validation to cover subagent MCP launch configuration explicitly. Add assertions that tracked plugin agent specs do not use a bare `nexus-mcp` command, and that installed `.codex/agents` use the installer runtime plus absolute `dist/mcp/server.js` path. Add a doctor/validation signal for malformed installed agent MCP blocks. Rationale: the bug only manifested in spawned subagents, so config-level coverage on both source and installed artifacts is the right permanent guardrail. Rejected alternatives: (1) rely only on manual reproduction with spawned agents — rejected because it is slow, brittle, and hard to automate in CI; (2) add only README guidance — rejected because documentation cannot prevent silent regression in generated config; (3) add only one source-side test — rejected because the real failure happened after installation, so both source and installed outputs must be checked.

plan.research_summary: Local repo evidence: the installer intentionally writes .codex/config.toml with an absolute runtime command plus the installed @moreih29/nexus-core dist/mcp/server.js path, explicitly avoiding PATH-dependent npx/nexus-mcp launch. However every generated non-lead subagent TOML under plugins/codex-nexus/agents hardcodes [mcp_servers.nx] command = "nexus-mcp" plus disabled_tools. In this repo, `command -v nexus-mcp` returns nothing, while node_modules/.bin/nexus-mcp and node_modules/@moreih29/nexus-core/dist/mcp/server.js do exist. README also promises that nx MCP is wired through the installer runtime and absolute server path rather than relying on PATH. Official Codex docs say custom agent files are configuration layers and optional fields such as mcp_servers inherit from the parent session when omitted; Codex MCP docs define command/args/cwd on [mcp_servers.<id>] and note project/user config.toml is the canonical MCP config surface. OpenAI Codex docs: https://developers.openai.com/codex/subagents, https://developers.openai.com/codex/mcp, https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-reference. Similar public cases exist in openai/codex issue tracker where MCP startup fails with program not found / os error 2 when the configured command cannot be launched, e.g. issue #2945 and #4754.

The installer already knows the correct runtime command and absolute `@moreih29/nexus-core/dist/mcp/server.js` path.

plan.topic: Update codex-nexus for nexus-core v0.19.1 and deploy a release

issues.title: Choose the correct downstream change set for adopting nexus-core v0.19.1 without retaining conflicting MCP workaround behavior

analysis.summary: Upstream v0.19.1 removes the root cause that codex-nexus had worked around: generated Codex child agents no longer hardcode `command = "nexus-mcp"` and instead preserve the parent session's MCP launcher. Keeping our downstream child-agent rewrite logic would now fight the intended upstream inheritance model, create unnecessary divergence, and force source/install behavior that upstream explicitly says consumers can remove. However, our main-session install logic that writes `.codex/config.toml` with a runtime + absolute installed server path remains valuable and should stay, because it solves parent-session PATH dependence. Therefore the correct scope is: keep parent-session MCP installation behavior, drop child-agent-specific rewrite/normalization workarounds, and resync generated assets from upstream 0.19.1.

issues.decision: Selected approach: adopt nexus-core v0.19.1 as the new source of truth for Codex child-agent MCP behavior, keeping only codex-nexus's parent-session install wiring and removing downstream child-agent nx MCP rewrite logic. Rationale: upstream now intentionally preserves the parent launcher and explicitly says downstream Codex consumers can remove those workarounds; retaining them would unnecessarily override the inherited launcher model and increase maintenance burden. Rejected alternatives: (1) keep all local rewrite logic while also bumping upstream — rejected because it preserves divergence after the upstream fix; (2) remove all MCP wiring, including parent-session absolute-path install config — rejected because parent-session PATH-independence is still a codex-nexus responsibility and remains documented behavior; (3) postpone release until more upstream releases land — rejected because v0.19.1 directly fixes the issue we filed and the wrapper should align promptly.

issues.title: Implement the update: bump dependency, remove or simplify obsolete MCP rewrite logic, resync generated assets, and adjust validation/tests

analysis.summary: The implementation delta is clear from local code search: remove `scripts/lib/nx-agent-mcp.mjs`, stop calling its normalization helpers from install/sync/validate/tests, rerun `sync:core` after bumping `@moreih29/nexus-core` to 0.19.1 so `plugins/codex-nexus/agents/*`, skills, and lead instructions are regenerated, and update tests to assert the new upstream behavior (child-agent MCP stanza should not hardcode bare nexus-mcp and should preserve disabled_tools while allowing parent inheritance). Also update any validation assumptions and published-files list that only existed for the workaround helper.

issues.decision: Selected implementation: bump @moreih29/nexus-core to 0.19.1, run the official sync flow to regenerate publishable skills/agents/instructions, then delete the downstream child-agent MCP normalization helper and all call sites/tests that depended on it. Replace them with checks that match the upstream contract: generated child agents keep per-role disabled_tools under `[mcp_servers.nx]` but do not force a launcher, while installed parent config still points to the runtime plus absolute server path. Rejected alternatives: (1) hand-edit generated plugin agent files without rerunning sync — rejected because it fights the generated source-of-truth model; (2) keep the helper only for install-time doctor — rejected because it would keep dead code and misleading assertions; (3) skip validation changes and trust upstream blindly — rejected because the wrapper still owns packaging, install surfaces, and release safety.

issues.title: Prepare and execute the release/deploy path, including version bump, changelog/release notes, validation, tag push, publish monitoring, and post-publish checks

analysis.summary: Current published version is 0.3.9, so the next safe release is a patch bump to 0.3.10. This release should include the upstream alignment plus removal of obsolete workaround code and the new generated assets. Release execution should follow the repository checklist: full validation locally, version bump/changelog, commit, tag `v0.3.10`, push branch and tag, monitor `validate.yml` and `publish-npm.yml`, confirm npm shows 0.3.10, run a fresh install/doctor smoke check against the published package, and create/update the GitHub Release if the workflow does not do that automatically.

issues.decision: Selected release plan: publish codex-nexus 0.3.10 as a patch release. Reasoning: 0.3.9 is already the latest published version, and this change is a compatibility/bug-fix alignment with upstream v0.19.1 rather than a feature or breaking change. Execute the checklist-driven release path locally, then deploy through the existing trusted-publishing GitHub Actions workflow by pushing tag `v0.3.10`. Rejected alternatives: (1) reuse version 0.3.9 — rejected because npm already has it; (2) publish 0.4.0 — rejected because the scope is not a breaking feature release; (3) stop at code changes without deployment — rejected because the user explicitly asked to deploy.

plan.research_summary: Current published codex-nexus version is 0.3.9 on npm and GitHub. Local package.json is 0.3.9 and depends on @moreih29/nexus-core 0.19.0. Upstream nexus-core released v0.19.1 on 2026-04-22. Release notes explicitly say Codex child-agent specs no longer hardcode a bare nexus-mcp launcher and downstream Codex consumers can remove workarounds that rewrote child-agent mcp_servers.nx.command. PR #62 / issue #59 confirm the intended behavior: preserve parent nx MCP launcher and keep only disabled_tools in child-agent MCP blocks by default. Local codex-nexus still carries a downstream workaround: scripts/lib/nx-agent-mcp.mjs plus installer/sync/validation/tests that normalize child-agent nx MCP config. Release checklist in .nexus/context/releasing.md requires updating changelog, running bun install, bun run sync:core, bun run validate, bun test, node scripts/codex-nexus.mjs --help, doctor --scope project, npm pack --dry-run, and completing post-publish checks. Local npm CLI is not authenticated, but this repo has a trusted-publishing GitHub Actions workflow that publishes on tag push. GitHub release notes say downstream wrappers should reinstall/resync assets from @moreih29/nexus-core@0.19.1.

plan.topic: Update codex-nexus to upstream nexus-core 0.20.0 and ship a compatible release

issues.title: Decide the upgrade scope and versioning strategy for moving codex-nexus from nexus-core 0.19.2 to 0.20.0

analysis.summary: Specific execution request. No HOW spawn: npm metadata, upstream git diff, and local package structure provide sufficient evidence. Observed change surface from nexus-core 0.19.2 -> 0.20.0 is limited to generated Lead/plan-skill artifacts, with no dist/mcp or harness contract changes. Therefore codex-nexus can treat this as a compatibility sync + local wrapper release, not a broader redesign.

issues.decision: Selected: upgrade codex-nexus from @moreih29/nexus-core 0.19.2 to 0.20.0 and ship codex-nexus as a patch release (0.3.11), because the upstream delta is confined to generated Lead/skill artifacts and does not change installer CLI shape, package layout, or MCP server implementation. Rejected alternative 1: stay on 0.19.2 — dismissed because 0.20.0 is already published and contains spec/skill updates that this wrapper is supposed to track. Rejected alternative 2: treat this as a minor/major codex-nexus bump — dismissed because there is no new user-facing command surface or intended breaking compatibility; the work is an upstream sync plus wrapper hardening. Assumption: runtime compatibility remains within patch expectations because the upstream diff does not touch harness layout or dist/mcp server code.

issues.title: Decide how to sync upstream-generated Lead/skill artifacts while preserving local installer compatibility fixes

analysis.summary: Technical issue with sufficient local evidence; no HOW spawn needed. codex-nexus installer already has a local agent-MCP rewrite fix in progress, and codex-cli 0.122.0 was previously reproduced to reject agent TOMLs that rely on partial mcp_servers inheritance ('invalid transport'). Upstream 0.20.0 sync output changes only lead.toml and the three plan skills, so the safe path is to sync those upstream artifacts while keeping launcher resolution as an explicitly downstream install-time concern.

issues.decision: Selected: sync upstream-generated Lead and plan-skill artifacts from nexus-core 0.20.0, but preserve and complete the downstream installer strategy that rewrites installed agent MCP launchers to the resolved runtime path and installed nexus-core server.js path. Publishable source agents may keep upstream bare 'nexus-mcp' metadata, while the installed .codex/agents and installed plugin agent copies are rewritten to launchable absolute paths. Rejected alternative 1: rely on partial mcp_servers inheritance in installed agent TOMLs — dismissed because local runtime evidence shows codex-cli 0.122.0 rejects that shape with 'invalid transport'. Rejected alternative 2: ship installed agents with bare 'nexus-mcp' and no downstream rewrite — dismissed because codex-nexus explicitly promises PATH-independent launcher wiring and doctor/installer should verify real executable paths, not bare command assumptions. Rejected alternative 3: fork or locally edit upstream-generated Lead/skill text beyond the required sync — dismissed because the observed 0.20.0 upstream delta is exactly in those generated artifacts, so the wrapper should stay aligned unless a concrete downstream incompatibility is proven.

issues.title: Decide the release/readiness changes needed in codex-nexus before shipping the updated package

analysis.summary: Release workflow evidence is available locally: publish-npm is trusted-publishing on tag push after validate.yml, and releasing.md requires changelog, full validation, doctor checks, npm pack dry-run, and post-publish smoke verification. No strategist/postdoc spawn needed because this is procedural release execution, not a business-choice ambiguity.

issues.decision: Selected: perform the full release-readiness set locally — version bump, changelog update, dependency update, upstream sync, validation, doctor, npm pack dry-run, and release commit — then attempt the repository's normal publish path only if the environment is already configured for it (tag-driven trusted publishing and usable git remote auth). If that final external step is unavailable, stop at a verified release-ready state and report the remaining external blocker explicitly. Rejected alternative 1: change code without release metadata or changelog updates — dismissed because releasing.md treats version/changelog alignment as mandatory. Rejected alternative 2: treat local tests alone as sufficient and skip doctor/npm-pack smoke checks — dismissed because this repo's release checklist requires install-path and package-contents validation, not source-only tests. Rejected alternative 3: force a manual npm publish path regardless of the repository's workflow — dismissed because the documented release path is trusted publishing on tag push, so bypassing it would diverge from the project's own release contract unless the existing path proves unusable.

tasks.approach: Execute the required release commands, verify doctor in clean project/user installs, confirm installed agent TOMLs resolve to real runtime/server paths, and confirm no malformed-agent warning appears during Codex startup against an installed user-scope layout.
tasks.risk: The package can look correct in source but still fail in clean install paths or packaged form.

- release execution is attempted only after review and verification tasks pass
- if git remote/auth and release trigger are available, the normal tag-driven publish path is initiated
- if the environment cannot perform that external step, the exact blocker is captured for the final report

If the external trigger cannot be executed from this environment, stop cleanly with the blocker identified instead of forcing an alternate publish path.

plan.topic: Add codex-nexus CLI version/uninstall commands and cmux-oriented Codex hook notifications

issues.title: Choose the codex-nexus CLI surface for displaying version information.
issues.decision: Selected approach: support both `codex-nexus version` and the conventional global flags `codex-nexus --version` / `-V`, all printing the package version and exiting without additional prose. Rationale: the package already exposes `PACKAGE_VERSION`, implementation cost is tiny, and supporting both surfaces avoids forcing users to remember whether version is a command or flag. Rejected alternatives: only a subcommand (dismissed because global version flags are a standard CLI affordance and users explicitly asked command or argument), and only a flag (dismissed because a documented `version` command is discoverable and consistent with other small CLIs). Assumption: version output should be the codex-nexus package version, not the upstream Codex CLI version, because this CLI manages codex-nexus installation behavior.

issues.title: Design uninstall semantics that revert only codex-nexus-managed changes for user/project scope while preserving unrelated user settings.
issues.decision: Selected approach: add a codex-nexus-managed install state file per scope and make uninstall a hybrid of metadata-backed restoration plus safe structural fallback. On install, persist rollback metadata under the managed Codex directory (scope-local) for the exact surfaces codex-nexus mutates: prior values for config keys (`model_instructions_file`, selected feature flags, `mcp_servers.nx`), prior marketplace entry for `codex-nexus`, which `.gitignore` lines were newly added, and backup copies or existence markers for fully managed file trees we overwrite/remove (`pluginInstallDir`, `.codex/agents`, `.agents/skills`, `lead.instructions.md`, package-store codex-nexus install root). On uninstall, restore only those managed surfaces: remove codex-nexus hook commands from hooks.json while preserving unrelated hooks, remove or restore the marketplace entry, restore prior config keys/value presence from metadata, remove only gitignore lines we added, and delete or restore managed directories/files according to recorded prior state. If rollback metadata is missing (older installs), run a conservative best-effort uninstall that removes clearly codex-nexus-managed artifacts and strips managed hook commands while only deleting config fields when they still match the exact managed values we would have written. Rejected alternatives: full-file snapshot restore for merged files (dismissed because it would overwrite unrelated user edits made after install), and pure structure-based uninstall with no metadata (dismissed because it cannot reliably restore original state for keys/entries we changed). Assumption: files under the copied plugin/agents/skills/lead-instructions managed paths are codex-nexus-owned surfaces, so restoring/removing them is acceptable even if the user modified them after install.

issues.title: Decide how to implement cmux-oriented notifications and status messages using Codex hooks, based on currently available local evidence and minimal-risk integration points.
issues.decision: Selected approach: implement cmux integration inside the managed hook script rather than relying on undocumented session-log watching. Use only documented Codex hook events plus local cmux CLI evidence. Specifically: (a) on `UserPromptSubmit`, if running inside cmux (`CMUX_WORKSPACE_ID` present) and not disabled by `CODEX_NEXUS_CMUX=0|false`, call `cmux set-status nexus-state Running --icon bolt --color #007AFF`; (b) on `PermissionRequest`, send `cmux notify --title codex-nexus --body "Permission requested"` and set the same status key to `Needs Input` with icon `bell` and color `#007AFF`; (c) on `Stop`, send `cmux notify --title codex-nexus --body "Response ready"` and set status to `Needs Input`; (d) preserve existing Nexus tag/context behavior and git-policy hook behavior. The hook should swallow cmux failures silently so non-cmux environments remain unchanged. It may optionally ignore subagent events when an undocumented `agent_id` field is present, but the core behavior must not depend on undocumented fields. Rationale: repo code confirms codex-nexus currently wires no completion/input notifications; OpenAI Codex hook docs explicitly document `PermissionRequest`, `PostToolUse`, `UserPromptSubmit`, and `Stop`; local cmux CLI help shows native `notify` and `set-status` commands; and local opencode-nexus code demonstrates the exact desired icon/color/status conventions (`bolt`/`bell`, `Running`/`Needs Input`, `#007AFF`). Rejected alternatives: relying on Codex’s separate `notify` config as the only mechanism (dismissed because the user specifically asked for hook-provided behavior and notify only covers turn completion, not running state or approval prompts), and session-log tailing (dismissed because it is fragile and not a supported integration path). Assumption: `UserPromptSubmit` is early enough to represent the start of a running turn for cmux status purposes.

issues.title: Determine required tests and documentation updates for the new CLI commands and notification behavior.
issues.decision: Selected approach: add targeted automated tests plus README updates in both Korean and English. Tests should cover (1) `version` subcommand and `--version` flag output, (2) install→uninstall round trip for both `user` and `project` scope with pre-existing unrelated config/hook/marketplace/gitignore content preserved, (3) uninstall behavior when rollback metadata is absent but managed values are still present (best-effort path), and (4) hook-script cmux behavior for `UserPromptSubmit`, `PermissionRequest`, and `Stop`, using a fake `cmux` executable in PATH to capture invoked arguments without requiring the real app. Documentation should update help text plus README/README.en command examples, scope behavior, uninstall caveats, and cmux integration controls (`CODEX_NEXUS_CMUX`, cmux-only best effort). Rejected alternatives: documenting only in help output (dismissed because installer behavior and uninstall guarantees need a persistent reference) and relying on manual cmux smoke testing only (dismissed because notification regressions are cheap to catch with a stubbed command test). Assumption: CHANGELOG update is nice-to-have but not strictly required for this coding cycle unless the repo convention or release practice expects it alongside README updates.

plan.research_summary: Scanned existing knowledge (.nexus/context/releasing.md and memory/pattern-upstream-vs-downstream-triage.md) and repo code. Current CLI (scripts/codex-nexus.mjs) supports only install/doctor/help and already exposes PACKAGE_VERSION internally. install copies managed plugin/agents/skills, writes lead.instructions.md, merges config.toml (model_instructions_file, features.multi_agent/child_agents_md/codex_hooks, mcp_servers.nx), merges hooks.json by stripping prior codex-nexus hook commands and re-adding SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit/PreToolUse, merges marketplace.json by adding/replacing the codex-nexus entry, and for project scope appends ignore lines to .gitignore. There is no uninstall or rollback metadata today. Existing tests cover install, doctor, hook tag injection, and blocked git commands. Current publishable plugin hooks.json and buildManagedHooks only register SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, and PreToolUse. No cmux integration exists in this repo. Local installed Codex CLI binary is /opt/homebrew/Caskroom/codex/0.122.0/codex-aarch64-apple-darwin; --help shows built-in -V/--version for Codex itself. Binary string inspection indicates hook/event support beyond what this wrapper uses, including session_start, user_prompt_submit, pre_tool_use, post_tool_use, stop, permission_request, and hook output/status-related fields such as status_message plus terminal statuses like Working/InputRequired/Completed. Therefore missing completion/input notifications are not caused by this repo already wiring such events; the wrapper currently never installs hooks for them, so a local fix here is appropriate if we can safely target Codex’s hook schema.

tasks.context: Update the core CLI script and managed install artifacts so codex-nexus supports `version`, `--version`/`-V`, and `uninstall --scope user|project`. Install should persist scope-local rollback metadata for the surfaces codex-nexus manages, and uninstall should restore/remove only those managed surfaces while preserving unrelated settings where possible. Managed hook registration must be expanded to include PermissionRequest and Stop plus hook-script support for cmux status/notifications.
tasks.acceptance:

- Fresh installs write rollback metadata sufficient for later uninstall; older installs without metadata still get a conservative best-effort uninstall.
- Managed hook registration includes PermissionRequest and Stop in addition to existing events.
- Hook script performs best-effort cmux integration using `cmux notify` and `cmux set-status` when `CMUX_WORKSPACE_ID` is present and `CODEX_NEXUS_CMUX` is not disabled.

tasks.title: Add automated coverage for version, uninstall rollback, and cmux hook behavior
tasks.context: Expand the existing Bun test suite to cover the new CLI surfaces and hook behaviors. Tests should validate both metadata-backed uninstall and conservative uninstall behavior, plus hook-side cmux invocations without depending on a real cmux app.
tasks.acceptance:

- Tests fail if uninstall removes unrelated config/hook/marketplace content or leaves codex-nexus-managed artifacts behind.
- Tests fail if cmux hook integration stops invoking `set-status`/`notify` for the targeted events.
- Existing hook/tag and install tests still pass.

Add fallback uninstall coverage when metadata is absent. Extend test/hooks.test.js with a fake `cmux` executable on PATH that captures commands emitted by user-prompt-submit, permission-request, and stop hook modes.

tasks.title: Document new CLI commands and cmux notification behavior in README files
tasks.context: The public README files currently describe install and doctor only. Users need discoverable docs for version/uninstall and for the new best-effort cmux behavior/disable switch.
tasks.acceptance:
- README.md and README.en.md both mention version and uninstall commands with examples.
- Both READMEs explain user/project uninstall scope and that unrelated settings are preserved where possible.
- Both READMEs mention cmux notifications/status behavior and the disable environment variable.

tasks.risk: Docs may drift from actual CLI behavior if they are updated before code/test verification is complete.

plan.topic: Stop rewriting installed plugin bundle agent TOMLs while preserving Codex runtime behavior

issues.title: Should installer stop rewriting pluginInstallDir/agents and only rewrite runtime .codex/agents?

analysis.summary: Specific installer-scope issue. Evidence from scripts/codex-nexus.mjs shows project and user installs rewrite both pluginInstallDir/agents and .codex/agents. OpenAI docs place custom agent runtime config under .codex/agents or ~/.codex/agents, while plugin bundle manifest exposes skills/.mcp.json rather than agents. No repo code path was found that consumes rewritten pluginInstallDir/agents at runtime; identified consumers are installer rewrite itself, doctor checks, and tests.

issues.decision: Selected: stop rewriting pluginInstallDir/agents during install and preserve the plugin bundle agent TOMLs as distributed source, while continuing to rewrite runtime .codex/agents. Rationale: the verified Codex runtime surface for custom agents is .codex/agents / ~/.codex/agents, and no identified runtime path in this repo depends on rewritten pluginInstallDir/agents. This removes tracked-source churn and better separates publishable plugin source from runtime-installed agent config. Rejected alternative 1: keep rewriting both locations for symmetry; rejected because the symmetry serves only local installer assumptions, not a verified Codex contract, and it is the direct cause of repo churn/confusion. Rejected alternative 2: move project plugin install path now; rejected because the user explicitly asked to keep the rest unchanged and only stop plugin-bundle agent rewrite. Assumptions: preserved pluginInstallDir/agents remain acceptable because plugin manifest/runtime use of these files is not evidenced in docs or repo runtime code.

issues.title: What repo changes are required in doctor/tests/docs to align with preserved plugin bundle agent TOMLs?

analysis.summary: Specific repo-alignment issue. Evidence from doctor/tests/docs: doctor currently asserts 'plugin agents use resolved nx MCP launcher'; installer tests require pluginInstallDir/agents nx command/args be rewritten; README and releasing docs describe project plugin install dir as part of install output and smoke-test surface. These assumptions must be narrowed so preserved plugin bundle agent TOMLs remain bare nexus-mcp while .codex/agents and config/hooks/marketplace remain validated.

issues.decision: Selected: update repo verification and documentation to validate only runtime-managed surfaces for resolved nx launcher behavior, while treating pluginInstallDir/agents as preserved source/bundle content. Required scope: remove pluginInstallDir/agents rewrite from installer; remove doctor assertion that plugin agents use resolved nx launcher; update installer tests to expect .codex/agents rewritten but pluginInstallDir/agents left bare; update README/releasing guidance to state that plugin bundle copies into pluginInstallDir but its agent TOMLs remain source-shaped while .codex/agents are the rewritten runtime copies. Rejected alternative 1: leave doctor/tests/docs unchanged; rejected because they would falsely fail after the intended behavior change. Rejected alternative 2: delete pluginInstallDir/agents entirely; rejected because it changes plugin bundle contents and exceeds the requested minimal adjustment. Assumptions: preserving plugin bundle agents is still useful as distributed source/template content even if runtime consumes .codex/agents.

plan.research_summary: Reviewed local repo code, docs, and tests plus OpenAI Codex docs. Verified: project/user session config precedence comes from .codex/config.toml over ~/.codex/config.toml for trusted projects; plugin marketplaces are read from repo and personal marketplace files; custom agents are defined under .codex/agents and ~/.codex/agents. In this repo, installer currently rewrites both .codex/agents and pluginInstallDir/agents. Runtime-facing custom-agent surface is .codex/agents, while plugin bundle manifest exposes skills and .mcp.json, not agents. Current pluginInstallDir agent rewrite is consumed only by this repo's installer/doctor/tests/docs assumptions, not by an identified Codex runtime integration path. Releasing docs currently warn that project install can collide with tracked plugins/codex-nexus source. User-requested direction: keep plugin bundle agent TOMLs as source/bare nexus-mcp and stop rewriting them during install, while preserving .codex/agents rewrite and plugin behavior.

tasks.title: Stop rewriting plugin install-dir agent TOMLs and realign repo assumptions
tasks.context: Current installer rewrites both pluginInstallDir/agents and .codex/agents. The agreed change is to keep plugin bundle agents at pluginInstallDir (project: <repo>/plugins/codex-nexus, user: ~/.codex/plugins/codex-nexus) in their source/bare nexus-mcp form while continuing to rewrite only runtime .codex/agents. Repo verification and docs currently assume both locations are rewritten.
tasks.acceptance:
- installCommand no longer rewrites pluginInstallDir/agents TOMLs
- .codex/agents TOMLs are still rewritten to resolved runtime/node + server path
- doctor no longer fails on preserved plugin bundle agents
- installer tests cover preserved plugin bundle agents and rewritten runtime agents
- README/releasing guidance reflects the new behavior
tasks.approach: Update scripts/codex-nexus.mjs to stop rewriting pluginInstallDir/agents and narrow doctor checks to runtime-managed surfaces. Update tests and documentation to expect preserved plugin bundle agents and rewritten .codex/agents.
tasks.risk: Project-scope install still copies into tracked plugins/codex-nexus in this repo; this change removes agent-TOML churn but does not solve the larger path-collision design.

tasks.title: Run focused validation and archive the cycle
tasks.context: After the code/docs changes, validate the installer behavior and repo assumptions against the new contract.
tasks.acceptance:
- relevant tests and validation commands pass
- no remaining code/docs references claim plugin install-dir agents are rewritten runtime launchers
- cycle is ready to close and summarize
tasks.approach: Run targeted tests and validation commands, confirm preserved plugin bundle agents vs rewritten runtime agents, then archive the cycle.

plan.topic: cmux hook UX update and duplicate-hook mitigation

issues.title: Update cmux hook behavior so Running/Needs Input use cmux internal icon names and notifications show a snippet of the assistant output, ideally skipping a leading [Pre-check] block.
issues.decision: Selected approach: update scripts/codex-nexus-hook.mjs so cmux status pills use validated cmux internal icon names (`oct-zap` for Running and `oct-bell_fill` for Needs Input), and replace static notification bodies with a snippet extracted from hook payload text. The snippet logic should prefer assistant-response fields (for Stop and other interaction-style events), strip a leading `[Pre-check]` block when present, normalize whitespace, and truncate to a fixed preview length with a safe fallback string when no usable text is present. Rationale: this directly satisfies the requested UX while staying inside the existing hook integration surface and test file. Rejected alternative 1: keep static bodies like `Response ready` / `Permission requested`; dismissed because it loses the concrete response context the user explicitly asked for. Rejected alternative 2: try to parse only one exact payload field; dismissed because observed tests already show field-shape variation (`tool_input` vs `toolInput`) and hook payloads may differ by event, so a tolerant extractor is safer. Assumptions: Stop payload continues to provide a response-like field such as `last_assistant_message`, and PermissionRequest may require fallback to approval/request text when no assistant message is available.

issues.title: Mitigate duplicate hook effects in this repository so UserPromptSubmit and related hook effects do not surface twice when both config-layer hooks and plugin hooks are active.
issues.decision: Selected approach: keep the existing install surfaces in place for now, but add runtime duplicate suppression inside scripts/codex-nexus-hook.mjs so identical hook events from the same workspace/prompt/turn collapse into a single visible effect within a short time window. Rationale: current repo evidence shows two active sources for the same hooks — generated config-layer hooks in .codex/hooks.json and the local plugin source hooks in plugins/codex-nexus/hooks.json made reachable through .agents/plugins/marketplace.json — so users see duplicated UserPromptSubmit output even though the hook script itself is correct. A small runtime dedupe layer removes the double warnings/notifications immediately without changing packaging/install contracts. Rejected alternative 1: remove config-layer hooks and rely only on plugin hooks; dismissed because installer/tests/docs currently treat config-layer hooks as a managed surface and changing that would be a larger contract change. Rejected alternative 2: keep behavior as-is and document that this repo is special; dismissed because the duplicate warnings are already harming the development workflow and the user asked for a fix, not just an explanation. Assumptions: duplicate executions for the same hook event arrive nearly back-to-back, so a short fingerprint+TTL gate can suppress only accidental duplicates while leaving later legitimate repeated prompts intact.

plan.research_summary: Existing knowledge: .nexus/context/releasing.md warns that project-scope smoke tests must not reuse the repo root because install target <repo>/plugins/codex-nexus overlaps publishable source paths. Current repo state shows .codex/hooks.json contains managed UserPromptSubmit/PreToolUse/PermissionRequest/Stop hooks calling the installed script under .codex/packages/node_modules/codex-nexus/scripts/codex-nexus-hook.mjs. The project marketplace file .agents/plugins/marketplace.json points to ./plugins/codex-nexus, whose plugins/codex-nexus/hooks.json also defines the same hook events. scripts/codex-nexus.mjs installManagedSurfaces() copies the plugin into projectRoot/plugins/codex-nexus and also merges managed hooks into .codex/hooks.json. scripts/codex-nexus-hook.mjs currently uses bolt/bell icons, sends static notification bodies ('Response ready', 'Permission requested'), and emits additionalContext JSON for [run] in UserPromptSubmit. test/hooks.test.js covers current cmux behavior and can be extended to verify new icon names, message snippet extraction, and duplicate suppression behavior.

tasks.title: Implement cmux hook preview extraction, icon updates, and duplicate suppression
tasks.context: Modify the codex hook runtime so UserPromptSubmit/PreToolUse/PermissionRequest/Stop produce the requested cmux UX: Running uses the validated internal lightning icon, Needs Input uses the filled bell icon, notifications derive their body from assistant/output text when available (prefer skipping a leading [Pre-check] block and truncating to a compact preview), and duplicate hook executions from overlapping config/plugin registrations collapse to one visible effect. Update tests alongside the implementation. Because this repo's active .codex/hooks.json points to the installed package copy under .codex/packages/node_modules/codex-nexus, refresh the local managed hook script after source changes so the current workspace exercises the new runtime.
tasks.acceptance:
- scripts/codex-nexus-hook.mjs derives notification bodies from assistant/request text when available, strips a leading [Pre-check] block if present, truncates previews safely, and falls back gracefully when text is unavailable.
- Running status uses oct-zap and Needs Input uses oct-bell_fill.
- Duplicate back-to-back executions of the same hook event do not produce duplicate visible outputs.
- Automated tests cover the updated icon names, notification preview behavior, and duplicate suppression.
- The current workspace's installed hook runtime under .codex/packages/node_modules/codex-nexus is refreshed to match the updated source for manual validation.
tasks.approach: Edit scripts/codex-nexus-hook.mjs to add notification text extraction/normalization helpers, use oct-zap and oct-bell_fill icons, and introduce a short-window fingerprint dedupe guard backed by an ephemeral temp-file cache keyed by workspace/project. Extend test/hooks.test.js to cover icon names, response preview extraction, [Pre-check] stripping, and duplicate suppression. After implementation, refresh the local installed hook script used by this workspace so manual cmux behavior matches source.
tasks.risk: Over-aggressive dedupe could suppress legitimate repeated prompts if the fingerprint or TTL is too broad; preview extraction could also miss actual payload fields if the hook payload shape varies more than expected.

tasks.title: Verify updated cmux hook behavior and duplicate suppression
tasks.context: Run the relevant automated tests and targeted manual checks for the updated hook runtime. Verification should confirm the new icon names, preview extraction (including [Pre-check] skipping), duplicate suppression, and that the current workspace can exercise the refreshed installed hook script.
tasks.acceptance:
- The hook tests pass after the implementation changes.
- Verification confirms duplicate back-to-back hook invocations collapse to one visible effect while normal single invocations still work.
- Verification confirms the notification body preview uses assistant text and skips a leading [Pre-check] block when present.
tasks.approach: Execute the focused hook test suite plus any targeted command checks needed to validate the refreshed local installed script behavior. Confirm no regression in existing hook JSON outputs.
tasks.risk: A passing unit test could still miss a real Codex payload shape difference, so verification should explicitly note which fields were exercised.

plan.topic: codex 0.124.0~0.125.0에 맞춘 codex-nexus P1 구현 계획

issues.title: P1 구현 방식: 구버전 호환을 유지하면서 hooks 설정 surface를 modernize하고 tool 관측 범위를 확장할 것인가, 그리고 어떤 단계로 적용할 것인가?

analysis.summary: Lead 검토: 현재 codex-nexus의 핵심 계약은 (1) installer가 .codex/hooks.json을 병합하고 uninstall 시 기존 사용자 hook 설정을 복원한다는 점, (2) publishable plugin bundle은 plugins/codex-nexus 아래를 source of truth로 유지한다는 점, (3) 설치된 runtime 경로와 publishable source 경로를 분리해 다룬다는 점이다. 따라서 P1은 upstream의 stable hooks 방향을 바로 강제 전환하기보다, hooks surface를 추상화한 뒤 stable config surface와 legacy hooks.json을 병행 지원하는 단계적 migration이 안전하다. 또한 hook event coverage 확대는 Bash-only matcher와 command-only parsing에서 벗어나 tool/event-aware parsing 계층을 먼저 만든 뒤 apply_patch, MCP, 필요 시 PostToolUse를 순차 추가해야 기존 cmux/permission UX를 덜 깨뜨린다. HOW subagent는 이번 턴에 생략했다. 이유: 사용자가 subagent delegation을 요청하지 않았고, 로컬 코드/테스트/공식 릴리스 근거만으로도 비교 가능한 옵션 도출이 가능하다.

issues.decision: P1은 즉시 전환이 아니라 호환 계층 기반의 단계적 적용으로 진행한다. 2026-04-23의 Codex 0.124.0 hooks stable 변경을 반영하되, 기존 사용자와 구버전 Codex 호환을 위해 managed hook spec을 추상화하고 stable hooks surface와 legacy .codex/hooks.json 병행 지원을 도입한다. tool 관측 범위 확장은 Bash-only 구조에서 input normalization 계층을 만든 뒤 apply_patch, MCP, 필요 시 PostToolUse 순으로 넓힌다. uninstall/restore/doctor 계약은 기존 사용자 설정 보존을 우선한다.

plan.research_summary: 공식 릴리스 기준으로 2026-04-23의 Codex 0.124.0에서 hooks가 stable로 올라가고 config.toml/requirements.toml 관리 및 MCP/apply_patch/long-running Bash 관측 범위가 강화되었다. 2026-04-24의 0.125.0에서는 permission profile/app-server/plugin 원격 기능이 보강되었으나, P1 범위와 직접 연결되는 핵심은 hooks modernisation과 tool coverage 확대다. 로컬 코드 확인 결과 codex-nexus는 현재 installer에서 .codex/hooks.json을 직접 병합하고(scripts/codex-nexus.mjs), publishable plugin hooks.json도 npx self-pin 방식으로 유지한다(plugins/codex-nexus/hooks.json). README 역시 hooks.json 병합을 현재 동작 원칙으로 문서화한다. hook script는 Bash command 중심으로 동작하며 apply_patch/MCP/PostToolUse 계열 처리가 없다(scripts/codex-nexus-hook.mjs). 테스트는 install/uninstall 시 hooks.json merge/restore/best-effort cleanup을 강하게 검증하고 있으며, 기존 사용자 설정 보존이 핵심 계약이다(test/installer.test.js). prior history에는 동일 주제 의사결정 기록이 없다. 메모 패턴상 upstream vs downstream triage를 먼저 해야 하며, 이번 P1은 wrapper/installer/integration 레이어 이슈이므로 downstream 로컬 변경이 중심이다.

tasks.title: Hook surface abstraction과 dual-surface install/uninstall/doctor 기반 추가
tasks.context: 현재 codex-nexus는 installer에서 .codex/hooks.json을 직접 병합하고 uninstall/restore/best-effort cleanup도 이 surface에 강하게 기대고 있다. P1에서는 managed hook spec을 공통 구조로 추상화하고, Codex 0.124.0의 stable hooks 방향을 반영할 수 있는 writer를 추가하되 legacy .codex/hooks.json fallback을 유지해야 한다. 기존 사용자 설정 보존과 rollback metadata 계약은 깨지면 안 된다.
tasks.acceptance:
- installer가 managed hook spec을 단일 소스에서 생성한다.
- 지원 가능한 최신 Codex surface에서는 stable hooks 설정을 쓸 수 있고, 미지원/불확실 환경에서는 기존 .codex/hooks.json fallback이 유지된다.
- uninstall restore와 metadata 없는 best-effort cleanup이 codex-nexus가 관리한 hook 조각만 제거하고 사용자 기존 hook 설정은 보존한다.
- doctor가 stable surface와 legacy hooks.json 중 실제 사용 중인 구성을 진단할 수 있다.
- 기존 install/uninstall 계약을 깨지 않는 회귀 테스트가 추가되거나 갱신된다.
tasks.approach: scripts/codex-nexus.mjs에서 managed hook 정의를 output-format 독립 구조로 분리하고, stable hooks surface writer와 legacy hooks.json writer를 도입한다. install 시 환경/버전/지원 여부에 따라 stable surface를 우선 적용하되 불확실하거나 미지원이면 기존 hooks.json 병합을 유지한다. uninstall/restore/best-effort cleanup, doctor, validate 경로를 모두 이 추상화 위로 올린다.
tasks.risk: Codex stable hooks 설정 surface의 실제 파일/스키마 차이가 예상과 다를 경우 adapter 분기와 smoke test 보강이 필요하다.

tasks.title: Hook runtime normalization과 apply_patch/MCP 관측 확장
tasks.context: 현재 hook script는 Bash command 문자열 중심으로 동작하며 PreToolUse/PermissionRequest matcher도 Bash에 묶여 있다. P1에서는 Bash-only 구조에서 벗어나 tool/event 공통 정규화 계층을 만들고 Codex 0.124.0에서 넓어진 관측 범위를 최소한 apply_patch와 MCP까지 확장해야 한다. 단, 기존 cmux 상태/알림과 permission deny 동작은 유지돼야 한다.
tasks.acceptance:
- hook script가 Bash 외 이벤트 payload도 공통 normalize해서 처리할 수 있다.
- apply_patch와 MCP 관련 이벤트에서 cmux 상태/알림 또는 정책 판단에 필요한 입력을 읽을 수 있다.
- 기존 Bash 기반 permission deny 규칙과 cmux Running/Needs Input 흐름이 유지된다.
- hook event coverage에 대한 테스트가 추가되어 Bash, apply_patch, MCP 케이스를 검증한다.
tasks.approach: scripts/codex-nexus-hook.mjs에 tool/event payload normalization 계층을 추가하고, Bash/ApplyPatch/MCP 이벤트에서 공통적으로 command/description/output/tool kind를 읽도록 바꾼다. installer의 managed hook spec도 이에 맞춰 matcher 또는 event wiring을 확장한다. PostToolUse는 optional 확장 포인트로 남기되 최소 목표는 apply_patch와 MCP 관측 및 기존 Bash 동작 유지다.
tasks.risk: upstream hook payload shape가 이벤트 종류별로 다를 수 있어 overly strict parser를 만들면 최신/구버전 중 하나가 깨질 수 있다.

tasks.title: Dual-surface compatibility와 expanded hook coverage 회귀 검증
tasks.context: P1 구현은 install/uninstall/doctor 계약과 hook runtime 동작을 동시에 건드린다. 기존 사용자 설정 보존, fallback cleanup, published-style install, Bash hook 동작이 유지되는지와 새 stable surface/apply_patch/MCP 지원이 추가되는지를 함께 검증해야 한다.
tasks.acceptance:
- install/uninstall/doctor 테스트가 stable surface 경로와 legacy hooks.json fallback 경로를 모두 검증한다.
- restore uninstall과 best-effort cleanup 테스트가 사용자 기존 hook/config/marketplace 설정 보존을 계속 보장한다.
- hook runtime 테스트가 Bash, apply_patch, MCP 이벤트를 검증한다.
- bun run validate, bun test, node scripts/codex-nexus.mjs doctor --scope project 기준 회귀가 없다.
tasks.approach: test/installer.test.js, test/hooks.test.js, test/plugin-layout.test.js, 필요 시 validate 경로를 확장해 stable surface 사용 가능 케이스와 legacy fallback 케이스를 모두 추가한다. published package 설치, restore uninstall, metadata 없는 best-effort cleanup, Bash/apply_patch/MCP hook payload 회귀를 검증하고 validate/test/doctor 명령을 통과시킨다.
tasks.risk: 테스트 fixture가 upstream payload나 stable hook schema 변화에 민감해 flake 또는 과도한 coupling이 생길 수 있다.

tasks.title: P1 호환성 변경 문서화 및 배포 체크리스트 반영
tasks.context: P1은 사용자에게 보이는 설치 surface와 호환성 정책이 바뀌는 작업이다. README, CHANGELOG, 배포 체크리스트에 최신 Codex 지원 방식과 legacy fallback 정책을 명확히 남겨야 이후 릴리스/지원 시 혼선이 줄어든다.
tasks.acceptance:
- README 한/영문이 설치 후 hook wiring과 backward compatibility 동작을 실제 구현과 맞게 설명한다.
- CHANGELOG에 사용자 영향, 설치/업데이트 변화, 호환성 포인트가 정리된다.
- 배포 체크리스트가 stable surface/fallback 검증 포인트를 포함한다.

tasks.risk: 구현 확정 전에 문서를 먼저 쓰면 실제 fallback 조건과 어긋날 수 있으므로 코드 확정 후 반영이 필요하다.

tasks.title: P1 문서 정확성 및 호환성 메시지 리뷰
tasks.context: P1 문서는 최신 Codex 사용자에게는 modernized hooks 동작을 설명하면서도, 구버전 사용자에게는 fallback과 제한을 정확히 안내해야 한다. wording이 부정확하면 지원 이슈와 잘못된 설치 기대를 만들 수 있다.

tasks.risk: 최신 Codex surface 명칭이나 지원 조건이 구현 중 바뀌면 문서 리뷰가 다시 필요하다.

issues.title: Define CLI command surface, scope behavior, and UX for selecting Codex default plus Nexus subagent models

analysis.summary: Code-verified CLI entrypoint is scripts/codex-nexus.mjs: parseArgs currently recognizes only command + --scope/--help; runCli dispatches doctor/install/uninstall/version. Existing scope prompts default to user for install-style commands, but model selection has higher risk of unintended global preference changes. The opencode reference defaults model configuration to project when scope is omitted. I judge the safest Codex command is `codex-nexus models [--scope user|project]` with interactive scope prompt defaulting to project and noninteractive direct mode defaulting to project. Direct flags should be `--agents <csv>` and `--model <slug>`. Include `default` as a selectable target for .codex/config.toml top-level `model`; exclude lead from subagent targets as requested.

issues.decision:
Selected: add a new `codex-nexus models` command that configures model overrides. Scope behavior: if interactive and `--scope` is omitted, ask for user/project with project preselected; if noninteractive direct flags are used without `--scope`, default to project. Target set: `default` plus architect/designer/postdoc/strategist/engineer/researcher/writer/reviewer/tester. `lead` is deliberately omitted because Codex Nexus controls lead primarily through instructions and the user explicitly excluded lead. Direct mode uses `--agents default,engineer` and `--model gpt-5.4`.

Rejected alternatives: (1) configuring lead too was rejected because it contradicts the request and risks breaking Nexus orchestration defaults; (2) using install command prompts for model selection was rejected because install should stay idempotent and non-interactive where possible; (3) defaulting to user scope was rejected for this command because unintended global model changes have broader blast radius than project-local changes, and the opencode reference uses project as the model-config default. Assumption: `models` is an acceptable command name; it is concise and does not conflict with existing codex-nexus commands.

issues.title: Define TOML mutation strategy and supported model catalog behavior for Codex config and agent files

analysis.summary: Explorer verified config/agent fields: default Codex model should be top-level `model` in scoped `.codex/config.toml`; subagent overrides should be top-level `model` in each runtime `.codex/agents/<agent>.toml`; lead remains untouched. Existing repo already uses TOML.parse/stringify for config and agent MCP rewrites, so the model command should use the same parser. Supported model catalog should prefer live `codex debug models` and include only catalog entries whose `visibility` is not `hide`, falling back to a curated Codex list only when explicitly injected in tests or when live discovery is unavailable in interactive mode with a clear error. Direct mode should validate requested model against discovered/injected catalog to avoid writing unsupported slugs.

Recommended `models` command with clack prompts, visible models from `codex debug models`, default target in config.toml, non-lead agents in runtime .codex/agents. Key additional risk: install currently recopies agent TOMLs, so model choices are lost on reinstall unless persisted in managed state/override file and reapplied after install. Also recommended `--targets` over `--agents` because `default` is not an agent, and targeted TOML edits to reduce churn.

issues.decision:
Selected: mutate TOML with `@iarna/toml` only. The pseudo-target `default` writes scoped `.codex/config.toml` top-level `model`; each Nexus subagent target writes scoped runtime `.codex/agents/<agent>.toml` top-level `model`. The command must not edit `plugins/codex-nexus/agents` because those are publishable source copied on install; runtime user choices belong in the scoped `.codex` layer. `lead.toml` is never targeted. Supported model discovery uses `codex debug models`, filters hidden catalog entries, and validates direct writes against visible slugs. Tests should inject a catalog so they do not depend on the local Codex binary.

Rejected alternatives: (1) editing plugin source defaults was rejected because install artifacts and user choices would be mixed and `sync:core` can overwrite generated plugin agents; (2) regex TOML edits were rejected because agent files contain large multiline strings and the repo already uses a TOML parser for similar mutations; (3) accepting arbitrary model strings was rejected because the request asks for Codex-supported model selection and unsupported writes would silently produce broken runtime config. Assumption: `codex debug models` remains the best local source of supported model slugs; if it fails, the CLI should explain that model discovery failed instead of guessing in direct mode.

issues.title: Define implementation/testing/documentation tasks for the model selection feature

analysis.summary: Implementation will touch CLI code, tests, and docs. Because direct code tests can inject a model catalog, no live Codex dependency is needed in tests. Essential acceptance: help/parse support new command; direct mode updates default and multiple subagent agent TOMLs; lead excluded/rejected; unsupported model rejected; docs list new command. A tester task should run targeted tests plus validate. Docs are README.md/README.en.md and CHANGELOG.md. Need beware .codex local artifacts: only temp test homes/repos should be mutated.

issues.decision:
Selected: implement in three execution tasks: (1) CLI/model-selection implementation in `scripts/codex-nexus.mjs`; (2) tests covering direct model configuration and validation, plus any install preservation checks; (3) documentation/changelog updates. Pair code work with runtime verification using Bun tests and validate. Keep interactive UX minimal and robust: use clack's existing `multiselect` for target selection plus `select` for model choice; this satisfies multi-select and Done/Cancel semantics without adding Ink/React. If later UX proves insufficient, a custom TTY can be introduced, but current dependency footprint favors clack first.

Rejected alternatives: (1) copying the opencode Ink implementation was rejected because this package does not currently depend on Ink/React and adding them for a small CLI command is unnecessary; (2) skipping docs was rejected because this is user-facing CLI surface; (3) relying only on manual testing was rejected because TOML mutation and target exclusion are regression-prone. Assumption: clack multiselect's submit/cancel behavior is sufficient for Codex version of the workflow even though opencode needed custom Next/Done/Cancel for provider/model grouping.

plan.research_summary: Existing knowledge: .nexus/context/releasing.md requires install/doctor/check commands and preserving publishable plugin source vs installed runtime copies; .nexus/memory/pattern-upstream-vs-downstream-triage.md says local wrapper/installer/config issues are fixed in this repo. History search for model/agent CLI decisions returned no prior cycles. Code verification: scripts/codex-nexus.mjs owns CLI parsing/runCli, scope resolution via resolveScopePaths, TOML config merge via mergeConfigToml, agent installation via installManagedSurfaces/copyDirectory/rewriteManagedAgentNxServerConfigs, and exported command helpers. Plugin agent TOMLs under plugins/codex-nexus/agents already contain top-level model = "..." fields for every Nexus agent including lead; user requested excluding lead, so only architect/designer/postdoc/strategist/engineer/researcher/writer/reviewer/tester should be configurable. Codex default model belongs to .codex/config.toml top-level model, evidenced by `codex --help` / `codex exec --help` examples using -c model="o3" and --model <MODEL>. Current Codex CLI provides a primary model catalog via `codex debug models`; on this machine (codex-cli 0.125.0) visible slugs are gpt-5.5, gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-mini, gpt-5.3-codex, gpt-5.2, gpt-5.3-codex-spark, with codex-auto-review hidden. Reference implementation: ~/workspaces/areas/opencode-nexus/lib/agent-models.mjs implements scope resolution, multi-agent selection, model validation, interactive and direct mode; Codex repo currently only depends on @clack/prompts, @iarna/toml, and @moreih29/nexus-core, not Ink/React.

plan.topic: install-time model setup prompt and inherited agent defaults

issues.title: Install flow should offer to run model configuration after successful interactive install

analysis.summary: `runCli()` has separate `install` and `models` branches. The existing `modelsCommand({ interactive: true })` already performs the TTY model selector and returns a `formatModelsSummary`-compatible result. Therefore install should, only in interactive TTY mode and after a successful install, ask a yes/no confirmation and then invoke the same `modelsCommand` with the installed scope. Noninteractive install must remain unchanged. To keep tests deterministic, add runtime injection for the post-install prompt decision rather than requiring a real TTY.

issues.decision:
Selected: after successful interactive `install`, show the install summary, then ask whether to configure models now; if accepted, run `modelsCommand({ scope: options.scope, interactive: true })` and show its summary. Noninteractive install will not prompt or run model configuration. The prompt should default to no/false or otherwise be low-pressure to avoid surprising users after install; however, if the user chooses yes, it reuses exactly the `models` command TTY implementation.

Rejected alternatives: (1) always opening the model selector after install was rejected because install should remain predictable and not force additional setup; (2) adding separate install-specific model UI was rejected because it would duplicate the tested `modelsCommand` flow; (3) running model setup before install was rejected because the runtime `.codex/agents` files may not exist yet. Assumption: the prompt only needs to appear in TTY sessions, consistent with current scope prompts.

issues.title: Generated/published agent TOMLs should inherit top-level Codex model by default by omitting top-level model fields

analysis.summary: Generated publishable agents under `plugins/codex-nexus/agents` currently contain top-level `model` fields. Install copies those files to runtime `.codex/agents`, so default installed agents pin models instead of inheriting top-level config. Because `sync:core` overwrites generated agents, the removal must be implemented in `scripts/sync-from-nexus-core.mjs` after replacing agents. The same sanitizer can remove a top-level model key from all publishable agents, including lead, without touching multiline developer instructions or `[mcp_servers.nx]`. Existing model override command still writes top-level model when the user explicitly configures a target, and persisted overrides still reapply after install.

issues.decision:
Selected: strip top-level `model` from publishable agent TOMLs during `sync:core`, then update the current checked-in `plugins/codex-nexus/agents/*.toml` accordingly. This makes fresh installs inherit the scoped top-level Codex model by default. Explicit user overrides still work because the `models` command writes runtime `.codex/agents/<agent>.toml` top-level `model`, and persisted overrides are reapplied after install.

Rejected alternatives: (1) stripping only during install was rejected because publishable source would still encode defaults and validation/docs would not reflect intended behavior; (2) manually deleting model fields without sync post-processing was rejected because the next `bun run sync:core` would reintroduce them; (3) removing only non-lead model fields was rejected because the stated principle is inheritance by default and lead instructions already exclude lead from model selector rather than requiring a model pin. Assumption: absence of `model` in agent TOML inherits the parent/top-level Codex model as the user stated; if Codex changes that contract, the fallback is explicit model selection via `models`.

issues.title: Tests and docs should reflect install prompt behavior and inherited model defaults

analysis.summary: Tests should cover: fresh install produces runtime and publishable agent TOMLs without top-level model; explicit model override still writes model and persists/reapplies; install prompt can trigger model setup via injected prompt/model-selection hooks without real TTY; declined/noninteractive install does not run model setup. Docs should explain that agents inherit Codex default unless configured, and install may offer immediate model setup in TTY. Validation should assert publishable generated agents do not pin `model`.

issues.decision:
Selected: update installer/model-selection tests plus plugin layout/validation assertions, and update README/README.en/CHANGELOG. Tests should parse TOML rather than exact text and use runtime injection for prompt decisions. Documentation should say fresh agents inherit the scoped Codex default unless a user runs `codex-nexus models`, and interactive install can launch model setup immediately.

Rejected alternatives: (1) relying on manual TTY testing was rejected because install prompt regressions are easy to miss; (2) documenting only the explicit `models` command was rejected because install now surfaces the flow; (3) skipping validation changes was rejected because `sync:core` could reintroduce model pins unnoticed. Assumption: no release version bump is required in this implementation cycle unless the user later asks for PR/release again.

plan.research_summary: Existing knowledge: .nexus/context/releasing.md requires install/doctor/check/pack verification and warns project-scope smoke can mutate local artifacts; .nexus/memory/pattern-upstream-vs-downstream-triage.md says local wrapper/installer/config behavior should be fixed in this repo unless upstream-generated assumptions are the root. Current branch is feat/install-model-config-inheritance. tasks.json and plan.json were absent. Code verification: `scripts/codex-nexus.mjs` owns install/models flows. `runCli()` dispatches install, uses spinner, then `outro(formatInstallSummary(result))`. `modelsCommand({interactive:true})` already opens the clack TTY model selector. `installManagedSurfaces()` copies plugin agents to scoped `.codex/agents`, rewrites MCP launcher, then reapplies persisted model overrides. `plugins/codex-nexus/agents/*.toml` currently contain top-level `model` fields for lead and all subagents, so fresh install does not inherit top-level config. `scripts/sync-from-nexus-core.mjs` replaces generated agents from nexus-core and then runs downstream launcher compatibility fix, so any default model stripping must be encoded as sync post-processing, not a one-off manual edit. issues.title: Decide release/versioning and upstream alignment strategy for adopting nexus-core 0.21.0

analysis.summary: Explorer verified through local grep plus temporary @moreih29/nexus-core@0.21.0 nexus-sync that upstream now generates 8 subagents plus lead and 3 skills, with no strategist.toml. Impacted files include package.json/bun.lock/.mcp.json, generated plugin agents/skills/lead instructions, scripts/sync-from-nexus-core.mjs, scripts/codex-nexus.mjs, validation/tests, README/README.en/CHANGELOG. Failure modes if not aligned include stale docs/help, missing strategist agent at runtime, stale planning instructions trying to spawn Strategist, and weak validation because optional filtered lists can pass despite stale target references.

Architect recommended treating 0.21.0 as an intentional subagent-set change and releasing codex-nexus@0.4.0. Rationale: removal of strategist.toml changes observable installed artifacts and CLI model-target behavior, so a pre-1.0 minor bump communicates compatibility impact better than a patch. Architect also recommended preserving only downstream compatibility fixes with concrete rationale: default model stripping and runtime MCP launcher rewrite.

issues.decision:
Selected: adopt @moreih29/nexus-core 0.21.0 as an upstream-alignment release and ship codex-nexus as 0.4.0. The implementation should bump package.json and bun.lock, update plugin/runtime pins such as plugins/codex-nexus/.mcp.json and hook self-pins, run bun run sync:core, and accept the generated removal of plugins/codex-nexus/agents/strategist.toml plus regenerated Lead/agent/skill text. Keep only the downstream post-processing that has an existing compatibility purpose: stripping generated top-level model defaults so agents inherit scoped Codex defaults, and rewriting installed runtime agent MCP launchers to resolved runtime/server paths.

Rationale: local verification and explorer analysis show that nexus-core 0.21.0 no longer generates Strategist and updates planning/runner contracts around an 8-subagent model. Architect analysis judges this user-visible because installed artifacts and model-target behavior change. A 0.4.0 pre-1.0 minor release communicates that compatibility break better than a patch while still fitting the package's existing 0.x cadence.

Rejected alternatives: (1) releasing as 0.3.18 was rejected because it understates the removal of a published subagent/model target; (2) staying on nexus-core 0.20.1 was rejected because the wrapper's purpose is to track current upstream-generated contracts unless a concrete incompatibility blocks adoption; (3) manually retaining generated Strategist files or editing generated text by hand was rejected because it would create downstream divergence from nexus-core 0.21.0 without a proven runtime requirement.

Assumption: upstream intent is inferred from the published 0.21.0 package contents and generated output, not from a separate release-note statement.

issues.title: Decide downstream handling for removed strategist model target and stale persisted overrides

analysis.summary: Explorer identified scripts/codex-nexus.mjs as the highest-risk downstream runtime file: MODEL_AGENT_TARGETS still includes strategist, GENERATED_AGENT_TARGETS derives from it, help advertises strategist, and persisted model override reapplication can still target a missing strategist.toml after install. Failure modes include direct models --targets strategist failing with a misleading 'run install first' style missing-file error, --targets all expanding to a missing target, and stale persisted strategist overrides causing reinstall failure after managed surfaces have been written.

Architect recommended removing Strategist everywhere instead of keeping a stub or alias. Narrow compatibility should be limited to current-target semantics: --targets all expands only to supported current targets, stale persisted model-overrides.json entries for strategist should be ignored or cleaned without failing install, and direct --targets strategist should fail clearly as removed/unknown. A stub would preserve stale 0.20.x semantics inside a 0.21.0 generated system and violate upstream alignment.

issues.decision:
Selected: remove Strategist from the supported downstream runtime surface, while adding narrow migration tolerance for stale persisted model overrides. Concretely, remove strategist from MODEL_AGENT_TARGETS / GENERATED_AGENT_TARGETS and help output, make --targets all expand only to current supported targets, and make direct user input such as --targets strategist fail before writes with a clear removed/unknown-target message. During install or persisted override reapplication, stale strategist entries from older .codex/.codex-nexus/model-overrides.json should be ignored or cleaned so reinstall does not fail just because a target removed by upstream no longer has an agent TOML.

Rationale: upstream 0.21.0 no longer provides strategist.toml, so keeping it as a normal target is false advertising and causes runtime missing-file failures. However, persisted overrides are managed state from older codex-nexus versions; failing an install because of old managed state is a poor migration experience and can leave partially written surfaces. The right compatibility boundary is therefore strict for new user input, tolerant for old managed state.

Rejected alternatives: (1) keeping a strategist agent stub was rejected because it creates stale downstream-generated semantics and would not be supported by the 0.21.0 Lead/plan skill contracts; (2) aliasing strategist to another HOW role was rejected because there is no verified semantic replacement and it would hide an upstream removal; (3) letting direct strategist target fail only later as 'agent config not found' was rejected because the CLI can validate targets earlier and should give an intentional compatibility message; (4) failing reinstall on stale persisted strategist override was rejected because old managed config should not block adopting the new upstream agent set.

Assumption: no user-facing replacement role for Strategist is available in 0.21.0; business/strategy questions should route through the remaining Lead/HOW process rather than a hidden alias.

issues.title: Decide verification, documentation, and release-readiness scope for the 0.21.0 adoption

analysis.summary: Explorer found validation/tests/docs that need hardening: scripts/validate-plugin.mjs, test/plugin-layout.test.js, and test/installer.test.js hardcode agent lists with strategist but often filter missing files, so the suite can pass while stale target references remain. test/model-selection.test.js should add strategist negative coverage and --targets all/current-target coverage. README.md and README.en.md advertise strategist as a model target. CHANGELOG should record the compatibility impact. Final verification should include sync, validate, bun test, help, install/doctor smoke, and grep that strategist remains only in historical/changelog migration notes if intentionally retained.

Architect prioritized verification around hardcoded role drift, model override migration, generated-vs-local divergence, and user-visible breakage. Recommended commands: bun run sync:core, bun run validate, bun test, node scripts/codex-nexus.mjs --help, install/doctor smoke for project and user scopes, grep for strategist, confirm .mcp.json pins 0.21.0, and confirm installed runtime agents still have resolved MCP launchers and no default model.

issues.decision:
Selected: verification and documentation must explicitly encode the new 8-subagent contract and the Strategist migration behavior, not merely rely on broad tests that filter missing files. Update validation and tests so the expected generated/runtime agent set excludes strategist; add model-selection regression coverage for --targets all succeeding against the current target set, direct --targets strategist failing clearly without writes, and stale persisted strategist overrides not breaking reinstall. Update README.md, README.en.md, and CHANGELOG.md to remove Strategist from current target lists and record the compatibility impact. Final release-readiness verification should run the existing release checklist plus targeted migration checks: bun install, bun run sync:core, bun run validate, bun test, node scripts/codex-nexus.mjs --help, node scripts/codex-nexus.mjs doctor --scope project, npm pack --dry-run, temp install/doctor smoke for project and user scopes where practical, and grep for strategist so only historical changelog/migration mentions remain.

Rationale: explorer analysis shows existing filtered agent-list tests can pass despite stale references. Because this change removes a published target, tests/docs must verify both absence from the current surface and graceful migration from old managed state. The release checklist in .nexus/context/releasing.md already provides the baseline packaging/install verification; this release adds targeted coverage for the newly identified migration risk.

Rejected alternatives: (1) relying only on bun test after naive sync was rejected because the simulation showed tests can pass while --targets all still fails; (2) documenting only the dependency bump was rejected because users relying on Strategist need an explicit compatibility note; (3) treating grep results under generated local .codex/.agents artifacts as source failures was rejected because those are ignored local install artifacts, but package-source strategist references must be removed except intentional historical/migration notes.

Assumption: final npm publication/tag push is out of scope for this planning turn unless the user later asks for release execution; this plan prepares the repo for release readiness.

plan.research_summary: Existing knowledge checked: .nexus/context/releasing.md requires package/plugin/hook version alignment, sync:core, validate, tests, help, doctor, npm pack dry-run, and temp install smoke; .nexus/memory/pattern-upstream-vs-downstream-triage.md says generated upstream behavior belongs to nexus-core while wrapper installer/CLI/docs behavior is local. nx_history_search for "nexus-core 0.21 strategist" returned no prior cycles. Current repo state verified: main is clean at codex-nexus 0.3.17 and pins @moreih29/nexus-core 0.20.1; npm registry latest @moreih29/nexus-core is 0.21.0 published 2026-05-08, while codex-nexus latest is 0.3.17. Lead ran a temporary 0.20.1→0.21.0 package/generated-output comparison: 0.21.0 removes the strategist agent, changes Lead to 8 subagents, updates agent bodies and nx-plan/nx-auto-plan/nx-run skills, and leaves nexus-mcp/server packaging present. Lead also ran a naive sync simulation in a temporary clone: validate/tests can pass after bumping .mcp.json, but direct runtime behavior still fails because scripts/codex-nexus.mjs keeps strategist in MODEL_AGENT_TARGETS; specifically models --targets strategist and models --targets all after install fail because .codex/agents/strategist.toml no longer exists. Local grep found strategist references in scripts/sync-from-nexus-core.mjs, scripts/validate-plugin.mjs, scripts/codex-nexus.mjs help/model target list, test/plugin-layout.test.js, test/installer.test.js, README.md, README.en.md, and generated current plugin assets. Explorer analysis (agent 019e05ec-84f8-73d2-aa4c-ecc09ab2577e) independently verified impacted files and failure modes: stale docs/help, persisted strategist model override reinstall failure risk, stale planning instructions selecting missing Strategist, weak validator/tests due filtered optional lists. Architect analysis (agent 019e05ec-85e1-7062-836a-d2503ba7fe4d) recommended codex-nexus 0.4.0, full strategist removal with narrow compatibility for stale persisted overrides, direct strategist target clear failure, no stub/alias, and verification focused on sync/validate/tests/help/install-doctor smoke plus grep for remaining package-source strategist references.
